# SupaWave — AI Agent Reference

SupaWave is a real-time collaborative editing platform based on Apache Wave, running on Jakarta/Jetty 12.
It supports wave/wavelet/blip conversations with participant-aware operational transforms and a robot API
for bots that participate in conversations.

---

## Architecture

### Jakarta Dual-Source Pattern

The codebase has two source trees for runtime-facing classes:

- `wave/src/main/java/` — original source (may be stale for runtime paths)
- `wave/src/jakarta-overrides/java/` — Jakarta override (what actually runs)

**Rule:** When both paths have a matching class, the Jakarta override wins. Always edit the override.
Use `scripts/jakarta-wrong-edit-guard.sh` to catch accidental edits to the shadowed copy.

### Build

```bash
sbt wave/compile          # quick compile check
sbt compile               # full build
sbt test                  # build + tests
sbt run                   # dev server on :9898
```

### Tech Stack

- Java 17 + SBT 1.10
- GWT (client), compiled to `war/webclient/`
- Jakarta Servlet / Jetty 12
- MongoDB (optional overlay; file-backed stores are the safe default)
- Google Guice (dependency injection)

### Key Source Paths

| Area | Path |
|------|------|
| Server entry | `wave/src/jakarta-overrides/java/org/waveprotocol/box/server/ServerMain.java` |
| Servlet routing | `wave/src/jakarta-overrides/java/org/waveprotocol/box/server/rpc/ServerRpcProvider.java` |
| Robot event types | `wave/src/main/java/com/google/wave/api/event/EventType.java` |
| Event synthesis | `wave/src/main/java/org/waveprotocol/box/server/robots/passive/EventGenerator.java` |
| Robot base class | `wave/src/jakarta-overrides/java/com/google/wave/api/AbstractRobot.java` |
| Feature flags | `wave/src/main/java/org/waveprotocol/box/server/persistence/KnownFeatureFlags.java` |
| API docs servlet | `wave/src/jakarta-overrides/java/org/waveprotocol/box/server/rpc/ApiDocsServlet.java` |

Live API reference: `GET /api-docs` or `GET /llms.txt` on a running server.

---

## Robot API

### What a Robot Is

A robot is an HTTP server that Wave calls back with events. The robot registers a callback URL;
Wave POSTs JSON event bundles to that URL. The robot replies with operations or posts via the
Data API.

### Capabilities XML

The robot must serve `GET /_wave/capabilities.xml`:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<w:robot xmlns:w="http://wave.google.com/extensions/robots/1.0">
  <w:version>1</w:version>
  <w:capabilities>
    <w:capability name="WAVELET_SELF_ADDED" context="SELF"/>
    <w:capability name="BLIP_EDITING_DONE" context="SELF"/>
  </w:capabilities>
</w:robot>
```

`context` values: `ROOT` (root wavelet only), `PARENT` (parent blip/wavelet context), `SIBLINGS` (sibling blips), `CHILDREN` (child blips), `SELF` (the matching blip or waves the robot is in), and `ALL` (all wavelets). Multiple values may be combined as a comma-separated list, for example `SELF,SIBLINGS,PARENT`.

### Event Types

Common event types from: `com.google.wave.api.event.EventType`

| Event | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `WAVELET_SELF_ADDED` | Robot was added to a wave |
| `WAVELET_SELF_REMOVED` | Robot was removed |
| `WAVELET_BLIP_CREATED` | New blip created |
| `WAVELET_BLIP_REMOVED` | Blip removed |
| `WAVELET_PARTICIPANTS_CHANGED` | Participant list changed |
| `WAVELET_TITLE_CHANGED` | Wave title changed |
| `WAVELET_CREATED` | Wave created |
| `WAVELET_FETCHED` | Wave fetched via Data API |
| `WAVELET_TAGS_CHANGED` | Tags changed |
| `BLIP_CONTRIBUTORS_CHANGED` | Blip contributor list changed |
| **`BLIP_EDITING_DONE`** | **All users finished editing a blip — use this** |
| `DOCUMENT_CHANGED` | Blip content changed (fires during typing) |
| `ANNOTATED_TEXT_CHANGED` | Annotation changed |
| `FORM_BUTTON_CLICKED` | Form button clicked |
| `FORM_VALUE_CHANGED` | Form value changed |
| `OPERATION_ERROR` | Operation failed |
| `BLIP_SUBMITTED` | **DEPRECATED — never fires, do not use** |

### BLIP_EDITING_DONE

This is the correct event for AI bots that should process blip content after the user finishes typing.

**What it is:** A synthetic event generated by `EventGenerator` when all `user/d/` editing session
annotations on a blip have their end timestamps filled in — meaning no participant is actively editing.

**When it fires:** When a user finishes editing (Shift+Enter, click away, close editor). Wave updates
the `user/d/{sessionId}` annotation value from `"userId,startMs,"` to `"userId,startMs,endMs"`.
The EventGenerator checks all `user/d/` annotations; if none have an empty end field, it synthesizes
`BLIP_EDITING_DONE`.

**Why it exists:** The legacy `BLIP_SUBMITTED` event was never implemented in the open-source Wave
codebase — `EventGenerator` never generates it. `BLIP_EDITING_DONE` replaces it with a real
annotation-based mechanism. See commit history: feat(robot-api): add BLIP_EDITING_DONE event.

**How to use it:**

```xml
<w:capability name="BLIP_EDITING_DONE" context="SELF"/>
```

```java
@Override
public void onBlipEditingDone(BlipEditingDoneEvent event) {
    String content = event.getBlip().getContent();
    event.getBlip().getWavelet().reply("\n" + generateReply(content));
}
```

One subscription, zero annotation parsing, zero deduplication needed.

### Why NOT BLIP_SUBMITTED

`BLIP_SUBMITTED` maps to `DocumentChangedEvent.class` in `EventType.java` (kept only for
deserialization compatibility). `EventGenerator` never generates it. Any robot subscribing to
`BLIP_SUBMITTED` will never receive it. It is `@Deprecated`.

### Detecting Editing Manually (only if you can't use BLIP_EDITING_DONE)

If you are using a non-Java SDK and must detect editing completion yourself:

1. Subscribe to **both** `DOCUMENT_CHANGED` and `ANNOTATED_TEXT_CHANGED`.
   The EventGenerator has mutually exclusive code paths: annotation ops fire only
   `ANNOTATED_TEXT_CHANGED`, content ops fire only `DOCUMENT_CHANGED`. The "done" signal
   is an annotation change, so you need both.

2. On each event, inspect `user/d/` annotations on the blip:
   ```
   annotation name starts with "user/d/"
   annotation value format: "userId,startTimeMs,endTimeMs"
   ```
   - Still editing: value is `"vega@supawave.ai,1775485999253,"` (empty 3rd field)
   - Done editing: value is `"vega@supawave.ai,1775485999253,1775486001215"` (non-empty 3rd field)

3. **Do NOT check annotation presence** — `user/d/` annotations are permanent; they are never
   removed. Only the end timestamp being filled in signals completion.

4. Implement deduplication: track the last content you responded to per blip, skip if unchanged.

### Async Reply Pattern

Wave's callback timeout is short (~5 seconds). AI processing takes longer. Always:

1. Return HTTP 200 immediately from the callback POST (acknowledge receipt)
2. Process asynchronously (run LLM, etc.)
3. Post reply via the Data API:

```
POST /robot/dataapi/rpc
Authorization: Bearer {token}

[{
  "id": "op-1",
  "method": "wavelet.appendBlip",
  "params": {
    "waveId": "supawave.ai!w+abc123",
    "waveletId": "supawave.ai!conv+root",
    "blipData": {
      "blipId": "TBD_reply_1",
      "content": "\nYour reply here"
    }
  }
}]
```

Note: `content` must start with `\n` (Wave blip format requirement).

### Robot Registration

```bash
curl -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/robots" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $MANAGEMENT_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "username": "my-bot@supawave.ai",
    "description": "My bot",
    "callbackUrl": "https://my-bot.example.com/_wave/robot/jsonrpc?token=SECRET",
    "tokenExpiry": 3600
  }'
```

Response includes `id` and `secret` for Data API calls.

### Example Robot: gpt-bot

Source: `wave/src/main/java/org/waveprotocol/examples/robots/gptbot/`
Docs: `docs/gpt-bot.md`

Run: `sbt "runMain org.waveprotocol.examples.robots.gptbot.GptBotServer"`

### Common Mistakes

- Using `BLIP_SUBMITTED` — never fires, do not subscribe to it
- Checking if `user/d/` annotation **exists** — it is always present; check the VALUE
- Only subscribing to `DOCUMENT_CHANGED` — misses the "done" annotation change
- Parsing annotation value as positional fields in the wrong order — format is `userId,startMs,endMs`
- Responding synchronously for AI workloads — will timeout; use async + Data API

---

## Feature Flags

Feature flags are stored in MongoDB (or in-memory for dev) and managed via the admin API.

Defined in: `KnownFeatureFlags.java`

Current flags:

| Flag | Description | Default |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `lucene9` | Lucene 9.x full-text search | false |
| `ot-search` | Real-time search wavelets (replaces 15s polling) | false |

Flags not in `KnownFeatureFlags` can still be created via the API; `mergeWithStored()` merges
runtime-defined defaults with stored overrides. Check against the enum before assuming a flag exists.

---

## Code Style (summary)

- ASF Apache 2.0 header on new source files
- Java 17, 2-space indent, 100-char line max, K&R braces
- No FQN imports, use explanatory comments when needed, no mutable vars where avoidable
- Logger: `org.waveprotocol.wave.util.logging.Log`
- Jakarta override copy is authoritative — check `wave/src/jakarta-overrides/` before editing servlets

Full guidelines: `CODE_GUIDELINES.md`
